title: corydalis latiflora subsp. gerdae
intro: Corydalis latiflora subsp. gerdae, also known as Gerda's Corydalis or Gerda's Fumewort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its yellow or white flowers and broad leaves. Traditionally, Corydalis latiflora subsp. gerdae has been used in herbal medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, derived from its alkaloid compounds, including tetrahydroprotoberberine and protopine. The plant is believed to have health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting potential neuroprotective and antioxidant effects. Corydalis latiflora subsp. gerdae is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
